name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Your AI coding agent will happily ship a breaking API change. I built an MCP server to catch it.
articles.summary: Last month I watched Cursor confidently rename a field across an entire API, commit it, and open a PR. Clean diff, tests green, looked great. It had also just broken a mobile client and a partner integration that were still reading the old field name — and neither Cursor nor I noticed until much...

articles.title: The Memento Problem
articles.summary: Memento wasn't a great movie, but Alignbase's new Agent Proposals is a great solution to what I like to call "The Memento Problem": Agents on the front-line are grappling with the same questions & roadblocks every single session. articles.title: How to build your first MCP server in 10 minutes

articles.title: The 2026-07-28 MCP RC hardens the protocol and relocates the attack surface
articles.summary: The 2026-07-28 release candidate is the largest MCP revision since launch. articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.

articles.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
articles.summary: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.

articles.author: vzisiadis

articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
